title: myroxylon latifolium
intro: Myroxylon latifolium, also known as Palo Santo, is a tree native to Central and South America, particularly in the tropical dry forests of Mexico, Guatemala, and Honduras. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the essential oils extracted from its bark, which contains a high concentration of guaiacol, a compound with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Palo Santo include reducing stress and anxiety, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing relief from pain and inflammation, making it a popular herb for aromatherapy and traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Palo Santo include essential oil diffusers, candles, and teas, which can be used to promote relaxation, improve mood, and boost the immune system.
